hadiths.chapter: 48. WHAT IS RECOMMENDED OF SUPPLICATIONS IN
hadiths.ar:
وَّ رَّوَّى عَّم َّارُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ:  «
إَّلْحَّمْذُّ وَّ إَّلْعَّط َّمَّه ُّ وَّ إَّلْكِن ْزِي َّاءُّ وَّ إَّلْح َّن َّزُّوب ُّ وَّ إَّلْحِلْمُّ وَّ إَّلْعِلْمُّ وَّ إَّلْح َّلاَّلُّ وَّ إَّلْح َّمَّالُّ وَّ إ لْكَّمَّالُّ وَّ إَّلْن َّهَّاءُّ وَّ إَّلْق ُّذْرَّة ُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّق ْذِي سُّ  
وَّ   إَّلي  َّعْط ِي مُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّسْن ِي حُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّهْلِي لُّ وَّ إَّلي  َّحْمِي ذُّ وَّ إَّلس َّمَّاحُّ وَّ إَّلْح ُّوذُّ وَّ إَّلْكَّزَّمُّ وَّ  إَّلْمَّح ْذُّ وَّ إَّلْمَّن  ُّ وَّ إَّلْح َّي ْزُّ وَّ 
إَّلْق َّص ْلُّ وَّ إَّلس َّعَّه ُّ وَّ إَّلْحَّوْلُّ وَّ إَّلس ُّلْطَّان ُّ وَّ إَّلْق ُّو َّة ُّ وَّ إَّلْعِز  َّة ُّ وَّ  إَّلْق ُّذْرَّة ُّ وَّ إَّلْق َّي ْق ُّ وَّ إَّلز َّي ْق ُّ وَّ إَّلل َّي ْلُّ وَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارُّ وَّ إَّلط  ُّلُّمَّاب ُّ وَّ إَّلن  ُّورُّ  
وَّ إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا وَّ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ُّ وَّ إَّلْح َّلْق ُّ ج َّمِي عاً وَّ إَّلْأ َّمْزُّ كُّل ُّهُّ وَّ مَّا سَّم َّي ْث ُّ وَّ مَّا لَّمْ إ ُّسَّمِّ وَّ مَّا عَّلِمْ ث ُّ مِن ْهُّ وَّ مَّا لَّمْ إ َّعْلَّمْ  وَّ مَّا كَّان َّ وَّ  
مَّا هُّوَّ كَّان ِن   لِل َّهِ رَّب ِّ إَّلْعَّالَّمِي ن َّ إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى  إ َّذ ْهَّث َّ ن ِالل َّي ْلِ وَّ ج َّاءَّ ن ِالن  َّهَّارِ وَّ إ َّن َّا   ق ِى  ن ِعْمَّه ٍ مِن ْهُّ وَّ عَّاق ِي َّه ٍ وَّ ق َّص ْلٍ عَّط ِي مٍ 
إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى  إَّلن  َّهٰارِ وَّ هُّوَّ إَّلس َّمِي غُّ إَّلْعَّلِي م   وَّ إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى 
ي ُّولِح ُّ إَّلن  َّهٰارَّ ق ِى  إَّلل َّي ْلِ   و.« .  إَّلل َّهُّم 
ن ِك َّ ن ُّمْسِى  وَّ ن ِك َّ ن ُّصْن ِحُّ وَّ ن ِك َّ ن َّحْي َّا وَّ ن ِك َّ ن َّمُّوب ُّ وَّ إ ِلَّي ْك َّ ن َّصِي زُّ وَّ إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِك َّ مِن ْ إ َّن ْ إ َّذ ِل َّ إ َّوْ إ ُّذ ِ ل َّ إ َّوْ إ َّص ِل َّ إ َّوْ إ ُّص ِل َّ إ َّوْ 
إ َّط ْلِمَّ إ َّوْ إ ُّط ْلَّمَّ إ َّوْ إ َّج ْهَّلَّ إ َّوْ ي ُّح ْهَّلَّ عَّلَّى  َّ ي َّا مُّصَّزِّف َّ إَّلْق ُّلُّوب ِ   ي َّن ِّث ْ ق َّلْن ِى  عَّلَّى طَّاعَّي ِك َّ وَّ طَّاعَّه ِ رَّسُّولِك َّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ لأَّ ي ُّز ِع ْ  
ق َّلْن ِى  ن َّعْذَّ إ ِذ ْ هَّذَّ ي ْي َّن ِى  وَّ هَّث ْ لِى   ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلل َّي ْلَّ وَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارَّ ج َّلْق َّان ِ مِن ْ 
ج َّلْق ِك َّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّن ْي َّلِي َّن ِّى  ق ِي هِمَّا ن ِح ُّزْإ َّة ٍ عَّلَّى مَّعَّاصِي ك َّ وَّ لأَّ رُّكُّوب ٍ لِمَّحَّارِمِك َّ وَّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن ِى  ق ِي هِمَّا عَّمَّل اً مُّي َّق َّن  َّلاً وَّ سَّعْي اً مَّس ْكُّورإً 
ون ُّور.  
983   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مِسْمَّغٍ كِزْذِي ن ٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: صَّل َّي ْث ُّ مَّغَّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّرْن َّعِي ن َّ صَّن َّاج اً ق َّكَّان َّ إ ِذ َّإ إِن ْق َّي َّلَّ رَّق َّغَّ 
ي َّذَّي ْهِ إ ِلَّى إَّلس َّمَّاءِ  وَّق َّالَّ  -  «  جَّي ْث 
ن َّحْي َّق ِظ ُّ وَّ مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ لأَّ ن َّحْي َّق ِظ ُّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إُّجْزُّسْن َّا مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ ن َّحْي َّزِسُّ وَّ مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ لأَّ ن َّحْي َّزِسُّ إَّلل َّ هُّم َّ إُّسْي ُّزْن َّا مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ 
ن َّسْي َّي ِزُّ وَّ مِن ْ جَّي ْث ُّ لأَّ  ن َّسْي َّي ِزُّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إُّسْي ُّزْن َّا ن ِالْع ِن َّى وَّ إَّلْعَّاق ِي َّه ِ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن َّا إَّلْعَّاق ِي َّه َّ وَّ ذَّوَّإمَّ إَّلْعَّاق ِي َّ ه ِ وَّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن َّا إَّلس  ُّكْزَّ 
عَّلَّى إَّلْعَّاق ِي َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
A group of our companions narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} that He said:  
"My father {a.s} used to say this supplication after the morning prayer:  
'O’ He who is closer to me than my jugular vein! O’ He who intervenes between a 
person and their heart! O’ He who is in the highest view! O’ He who is unlike anything! 
And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing! O’ most generous of those who are asked and 
the most expansive in giving! O’ best to call upon, O’ most noble to hope for! O’ the 
most hearing of those who hear and the most seeing of those who see! O’ the best of 
helpers and the swiftest in reckoning! O’ most merciful of the merciful! O’ most just of 
judges! Send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, expand my 
provision for me, prolong my life, spread over me Your mercy, and make me among 
those who You use to support Your religion and do not replace me with others. O’ Allah 
{SWT}, You have taken responsibility for my provision and the provision of every creature, 
so expand Your abundant lawful provision upon me and my family, and protect us from 
poverty.'  
He would then say,  
'Welcome to the two recording angels. May Allah {SWT} greet you both. Record, may 
Allah {SWT} have mercy on you, that I bear witness there is no god but Allah {SWT}, alone 
without partner. I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and messenger. I bear 
witness that religion is as He legislated, Islam is as He described, the Book is as He 
revealed, and that the saying is as He conveyed, and that Allah {SWT} is the true clear 
reality. O’ Allah {SWT}, convey to Muhammad and the family of Muhammad the best 
greetings and peace.'  
He would conclude by saying,  
'I have awoken, and my Lord {AZJ} is praised. I do not associate anything with Allah {SWT}, 
nor do I call upon anyone alongside Allah {SWT}. I do not take anyone as a guardian other 
than Him. I have awoken as a servant, possessed, owning only what my Lord {AZJ} has 
granted me. I have awoken unable to bring good I hope for or repel harm I fear. I have 
awoken bound by my actions, and I am the poorest of the poor before Allah {SWT}. I wake 
with Allah {SWT}, I sleep with Allah {SWT}, I live by Allah {SWT}, I die by Allah {SWT}, and to Allah 
{SWT} is the resurrection.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
Ammar bin Musa narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} who said:  
"When you wake up in the morning and in the evening, say:  
'We awaken with the dominion, praise, majesty, greatness, power, forbearance, 
knowledge, splendor, beauty, perfection, magnificence, strength, sanctification, 
glorification, magnification, oneness, praise, generosity, kindness, magnanimity, 
glory, favour, wealth, might, power, capability, expansion, and contraction of the day 
and night, the darkness and the light, this world and the hereafter, all creation, all 
matters, and everything named or unnamed, known or unknown, past, present, and 
future - all belong to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of the worlds.  
Praise be to Allah {SWT} who took away the night and brought the day, while I am in His 
favour, wellbeing, and great bounty.  
Praise be to Allah {SWT}, who 'has in His ownership everything that dwells in the night 
and the day, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.' Praise be to Allah {SWT}, who 'merges 
the night into the day and the day into the night, brings forth the living from the dead 
and brings forth the dead from the living,' and 'He is the Knower of what is in the hearts.'  
O’ Allah {SWT}, by You we enter the evening and by You we enter the morning, by You we 
live and by You we die, and to You is the return.  
I seek refuge in You from being humiliated, or causing humiliation, going astray or 
leading astray, oppressing or being oppressed, acting ignorantly or being treated 
ignorantly.  
O’ Turner of hearts, make my heart firm upon Your obedience and the obedience of 
Your Messenger.  
O’ Allah {SWT}, do not let my heart deviate after You have guided me, and grant me mercy 
from Yourself, for You are the Bestower.'  
Then say: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, the night and the day are creations from Your creation; do not 
test me in them by making me bold in sins or riding upon forbidden acts. Grant me in 
them acceptable deeds, appreciated effort, and an unfailing trade.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 336 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 810 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 83, 
p. 188 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 337 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1570 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 83, p. 286 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 48, H. 982

hadiths.chapter: 48. WHAT IS RECOMMENDED OF SUPPLICATIONS IN
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Misma‘ Kardin that He said:  
"I prayed with Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} for forty mornings. Whenever he 
finished the prayer, he would raise his hands toward the sky and say:  
'We have entered the morning and the dominion belongs to Allah {SWT}. O’ Allah {SWT}, we 
are Your servants, the children of Your servants. O’ Allah {SWT}, protect us from every 
angle we strive to protect ourselves from and from angles we do not perceive. O’ Allah 
{SWT}, guard us from every direction we seek to guard ourselves and from those we do 
not foresee. O’ Allah {SWT}, conceal us with sufficiency and wellbeing. O’ Allah {SWT}, grant 
us wellbeing and the continuity of wellbeing and grant us the thankfu lness for this 
wellbeing.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 338 • Makarem al-
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 277 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 811 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 83, p. 190 • Mustadrak Al -Wasail, Vol. 
5, p. 92

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 48, H. 983

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
985  - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عُّمَّزَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّز ِي ذَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: س َّكَّوْب ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلس َّهْوَّ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ ق َّق َّال ا ن ِ   
« و » .
hadiths.en:
Ismail ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from 
his forefathers {a.s} that:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} was approached by a man who said:  
'O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, I complain to you about the whispers (waswasah) I 
experience during my prayers, to the point that I no longer comprehend what I have 
prayed, whether I have added something or omitted something.'  
The Messenger of Allah {saws} responded: 'When you enter into your prayer, strike your 
left thigh with your right index finger, then say: "In the name of Allah {SWT}, and with Allah 
{SWT}, I place my trust in Allah {SWT}. I seek refuge with Allah {SWT}, the All-Hearing, the All-
Knowing, from the accursed Devil."  
By doing this, you will strike him and ward him off, and he will be driven away from 
you.'". 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 984

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
986  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ رَّج ُّل  
ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ لَّق ِي ث ُّ مِن ْ وَّسْوَّسَّه ِ صَّذْرِى  س ِذ َّة ً وَّ إ َّن َّا رَّج ُّل  مُّعِي ل  مَّذِي ن   مُّحْوِح     ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه 
ي َّوَّك َّلْث   و  صَّاجِن َّه ً وَّ لأ 
وَّ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهُّ وَّلِى    مِن َّ إَّلذ  ُّلِّ  وَّ كَّن ِّزْةُّ ي َّكْن ِي زإً   »
وَّسْوَّسَّه َّ صَّذْرِى  وَّ ق َّص َّى ذَّي ْن ِى  وَّ وَّس َّغَّ رِر ْق ِى .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Umar ibn Yazid that He said:  
"I complained to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about my forgetfulness 
during the Maghrib prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: 
 'Pray it by reciting 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad'  (Surah Al-Ikhlas) and Say, O’ disbelievers  
(Surah Al-Kafirun).'  
So, I did as he instructed, and it took away my forgetfulness”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 338 • Falah Al -Sael, Part 1, p. 229 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 
1003 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 236 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 230 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 5, p. 687 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 985

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
987  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال إ َّوْ ن ِحَّصًى ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ ن ِي َّذِةِ 
ق َّي َّعُّذ ُّ ن ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Hamzah al-Thumali narrated from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"A man came to the Prophet {saws} and said:  
'O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, I am experiencing great distress due to the whispers in my 
chest, and I am a man who is a caretaker, in debt, and in need.'  
The Prophet {saws} said to him: 'Repeat these words:  
"I place my trust in the Ever-Living, Who does not die," and "Praise be to Allah {SWT}, Who 
did not take a wife or a son, and Who has no partner in His kingdom, nor a helper in His 
humiliation," and magnify Him with great magnification.'  
The man said that shortly afterward, he returned to the Prophet {saws} and said:  
'O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, Allah {SWT} has removed the whispers from my chest, 
settled my debt, and expanded my sustenance.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 986

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Abdullah ibn al-Mughira, it is said:  
"There is no harm if a man counts his prayers using his ring or by using pebbles that he 
holds in his hand and counts with them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 339 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1002 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 247 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 423 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 987
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٨٧

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Imam al-Ridha {a.s} said:  
"If you experience frequent distraction (waswasah) during your prayer, continue with 
your prayer and do not repeat it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 339 • Al -Wafi, V. 8, p. 999 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 988
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٨٨

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
990  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إِن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ إ َّن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال إ كَّان َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ مِم َّن ْ 
ي َّسْهُّو ق ِى  كُّلِّ ي َّلاَّب ٍ ق َّهُّوَّ مِم َّن ْ كَّي ُّزَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّهْوُّ .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
who said:  
"If you experience frequent distraction (waswasah), ignore it, for it will soon leave you. 
It is only from Satan”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 989
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٨٩

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
991  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال 
وَّ إَّلز ُّكُّوعِ وَّ إَّلس ُّح ُّوذِ زِي ص َّه َّ .   
وإلأ صل ق ى  إلسهو إ ن  من  سها ق ى  إلزكعي ي ن  إلأ ولي ي ن   من  كل صلاة  ق علي ه إلأ عاذة  ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلمع زب  ق علي ه إلأ عاذة ، ومن   
س ك  ق ى  إلع ذإة  ق علي ه إلأ عاذة ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلح معه  ق علي ه إلأ عاذة ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلي ان ي ه  وإلي الي ه  إ و ق ى  إلي الي ه  وإلزإن عه  إ ج ذ   
ن الأ كي ز، ق ا ذ إ سلم إ ي م ما ط ن  إ ن ه ق ذ ن ق ض .
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ibn Abi Umayr, from Muhammad ibn Abi Hamza said:  
"The Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: 'If a man is one who forgets or 
becomes distracted in every third part of his prayer, then he is among those for whom 
distractions are frequent.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 339 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 107 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, 
p. 1004 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 229 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 276 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 990
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٠

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
992   -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لِعَّم َّارِ ن ْن ِ مُّوسَّى :  «
ق َّح ُّذ ْ ن ِالْأ َّكْي َّزِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ سَّل َّمْث َّ ق َّا َّي ِم َّ مَّا ط َّن َّن ْث َّ إ َّن  َّك َّ ق َّذْ ن َّق َّصْث .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"The prayer is only to be repeated in five cases : purification (tahur), time (waqt), the 
direction of the Qiblah, the bowing (ruku'), and prostration (Sujood)." He then said: 
"Recitation (qira'ah) is a Sunnah, and the testimony (tashahhud) is a Sunnah, and 
Sunnah does not invalidate obligatory act (fardh)”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
The general rule regarding forgetfulness (Sahw) is that if someone forgets in the first 
two rak'ahs of any prayer, they must repeat the prayer. If someone doubts during the 
Maghrib prayer, they must repeat it. If someone doubts during the Fajr prayer, they  
must repeat it.  
If someone doubts during the Jumu’ah prayer, they must repeat it. However, in the 
second and third rak'ahs, or in the third and fourth rak'ahs, they should follow the 
majority (what they believe is more likely correct). After concluding the prayer, they 
should complete what they think may have been missing.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 339 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 284 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 
2, p. 152 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 44 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 919 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 470 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 
6, p. 91 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 401 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 234 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 136 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 991
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩١

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
993 - وَّ مَّعْن َّى إَّلْح َّن َّزِ إَّل َّذ ِى  رُّوِى .  
إ ن ما هو  ق ى  إلي لاب  وإلأ رن غ لأ ق ى  إلأ ولي ي ن  . 
ولأ ي ح ث  سح ذي ا إلسهو إ لأ على من  ق عذ ق ى  جال ق ي امه، إ و ق ام ق ى  جال ق عوذة، إ و ي زك  إلي س هذ، إ و لم ي ذر ر إذ إ و ن ق ض، 
وهما ن عذ إلي سلي م ق ى  إلز ي اذة  وإلن ق صان  .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said to Ammar ibn Musa:  
"O’ Ammar, I will sum up all forgetfulness for you in two words: Whenever you are in 
doubt, follow the majority (the greater number). When you finish your prayer, complete 
what you thought you may have missed”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 340 • Wasail Al -Shia, Vol. 8, p. 212 • Al -Fusul al -
Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 114 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 170 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 992
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٢

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
The meaning of the narration that was reported:  
"The jurist (faqih) does not repeat the prayer,"  
[AL SADUQ] 
This applies to the third and fourth units of prayer, not the first two.  
And the prostrations of forgetfulness (Sajdah al -Sahw) are obligatory only for those 
who sat while they should have been standing, or stood when they should have been 
sitting, or omit ted the Tashahhud, or were uncertain whether they had added or 
omitted something. The prostrations are done after the Tasleem (salutation) in cases 
of addition or omission. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 993
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٣

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
995  -  وَّ إ َّم َّا جَّذِي ث ُّ صَّق ْوَّإن َّ ن ْن ِ مِهْزَّإن َّ إَّلْح َّم َّالِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: وَّ سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ سَّح ْذَّي َّى ِ إَّلس َّهْوِ 
ق َّق َّال .    
ق ا ن ى  إ ق ي ى  ن ه ق ى  جال إلي ق ي ه  .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"The two prostrations of forgetfulness (Sajdah al -Sahw) are after the Tasleem 
(salutation) and before speaking." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 341 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 195 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 380 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 994 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 208 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 994
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٤

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
996  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ عَّم َّار  إَّلس َّان َّاطِى :  عَّن ْ سَّح ْذَّي َّى ِ إَّلس َّهْوِ هَّلْ ق ِي هِمَّا ي َّكْن ِي ز  إ َّوْ ي َّسْن ِي ح  ق َّق َّال 
ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  سَّهَّا هُّوَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامَّ كَّن  َّزَّ إ ِذ َّإ سَّح َّذَّ وَّ   إ ِذ َّإ رَّق َّغَّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ لِي َّعْلَّمَّ مَّن ْ ج َّلْق َّهُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّذْ سَّهَّا ق َّلَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّسَّن ِّحَّ 
ق ِي هِمَّا وَّ لأَّ ق ِي هِمَّا ي َّس َّه ُّذ  ن َّعْذَّ إَّلس َّح ْذَّي َّي ْن ِ .
hadiths.en:
As for the narration of Safwan ibn Mehran al -Jammal from Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
that He said: "I asked him about the two prostrations of forgetfulness (Sajdat al-Sahw),  
Imam {a.s} said: 'If you have made a mistake by reducing something in your prayer, then 
[perform the prostrations] before the Tasleem. But if you have added something, then 
[perform the prostrations] after the Tasleem.'" 
[AL SADUQ] 
I give this ruling in the state of Taqiya. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 341 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 208 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 995
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٥

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
997  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال مِ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ ن ِالل َّهِ وَّ صَّل َّى  
إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّى مُّحَّم َّذٍ وَّ إ لِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ مِ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ ن ِالل َّهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ إ َّي  ُّهَّا إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ وَّ رَّجْمَّه ُّ 
إَّلل َّهِ وَّ ن َّزَّكَّاي ُّهُّ .    
ومن  س ك  ق ى  إ ذ إن ه وق ذ إ ق ام إلصلاة  ق لي مض ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلأ ق امه  ن عذ ما كن ز ق لي مض ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلي كن ي ز ن عذ ما ق زإ  
ق لي مض ، ومن  س ك  ق ى  إلق زإءة  ن عذ ما ركغ ق لي مض ، ومن   س ك  ق ى  إلزكوع ن عذ ما سح ذ ق لي مض ، وكل س ى  س ك  ق ي ه وق ذ ذج ل 
ق ى  جاله  إ ج زى ق لي مض ، ولأ ي لي ق ث  إ لى إلس ك  إ لأ إ ن  ي سي ي ق ن ، ومن  إسي ي ق ن  إ ن ه ي زك  إلأ ذ إن  وإلأ ق امه  ي م ذ كز ولم ي كن 
ق زإ  عامه  إلسورة  ق لا ن ا س ن ي زك  إلأذ إن  ق لي صل على إلن ن ى  صلى إلله علي ه وإ له   ولي ق ل: ق ذ ق امث  إلصلاة 
ومن  إسي ي ق ن  إ ن ه لم ي كن ز ي كن ي زة  إلأق ي ي اح ق لي عذ صلاي ه وكي ف  له ن ا ن  ي سي ي ق ن  .
hadiths.en:
Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam {a.s} about the two prostrations of forgetfulness 
(Sajdat al -Sahw), and whether there is any Takbir (saying "Allahu Akbar") or Tasbih 
(glorification) in them.  
He (Imam Ja'far Al-Sadiq) replied: "No, they are simply two prostrations.  
If the one who forgot is the Imam, then he should say Takbir when he prostrates and 
when he raises his head, so that those behind him know that he has made a mistake. 
There is no need for him to say Tasbih in them, and there is no need for any Tashahhud 
(testification) after the two prostrations."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 341 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 196 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 381 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 106 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 995 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 235 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 221 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 996
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٦

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"In the two prostrations of forgetfulness, you should say: “In the name of Allah {SWT} and 
by Allah {SWT}, and may Allah {SWT} send blessings upon Muhammad {saws} and the family 
of Muhammad {saws}. 
He said: "And I heard him say another time: In the name of Allah {SWT} and by Allah {SWT}, 
peace be upon you, O’ Prophet {saws}, and the mercy of Allah {SWT} and His {SWT} blessings.” 
[AL SADUQ] 
If someone doubts his call to prayer (adhan) after he has already begun the prayer, he 
should continue.  
If someone doubts the iqamah after he has said the Takbir, he should continue.  
If someone doubts the Takbir after he has recited, he should continue.  
If someone doubts the recitation after he has bowed (ruku'), he should continue.  
If someone doubts the bowing after he has prostrated (sujud), he should continue.  
If someone doubts anything after he has entered another stage of prayer, he should 
continue and not pay attention to the doubt unless he is certain.  
If someone is certain that he missed the adhan and the iqamah, but has not yet read 
the whole Surah, there is no harm in skipping the adhan. He should pray upon the 
Prophet {saws} and say, Qad qamat as-salah [The prayer has been established].  
If someone is certain he has not made the Takbir al -Ihram (the opening Takbir), he 
should repeat the prayer. But how can he be certain?"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 342 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 196 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 8, p. 234 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 220 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 997
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٩٩٧

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
999  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّسِى َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّكَّن ِّزَّ جَّي  َّى ذَّج َّلَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّق َّال 
ق ِى  ن ِي  َّي ِهِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّكَّن ِّز .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"A person does not forget the Takbira al-Ihram (the opening Takbir)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 343 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 915 • Ithbāt al-Hudāt, Part 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 998

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1000  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ َّجْمَّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصْزٍ إَّلْن َّز َّن ْطِى  ُّ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّسِى َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّكَّن ِّزَّ ي َّكْن ِي زَّة َّ إَّلأِق ْي ِي َّاحِ 
جَّي  َّى كَّن  َّزَّ لِلز ُّكُّوعِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man who 
forgot to say the Takbir (the opening Takbir) until he had entered the prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Was it not in his intention to say the Takbir?" He replied: "Yes."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Then let him continue with his prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 343 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 144 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 352 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 915 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 15 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 999

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1001  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال -  ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ رَّج ُّل  ن َّسِى َّ إ َّو َّلَّ ي َّكْن ِي زَّة ِ إَّلأِق ْي ِي َّاحِ ق َّق َّال 
ذ َّكَّزَّهَّا ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلز ُّكُّوعِ كَّن  َّزَّ ي ُّم َّ ق َّزَّإ َّ ي ُّم َّ رَّكَّغَّ وَّ إ ِن ْ ذ َّكَّزَّهَّا ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ كَّن  َّزَّهَّا ق ِى  مَّق َّامِهِ ق ِى    مَّوْص ِغِ إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زِ ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ 
إ َّوْ ن َّعْذَّ  إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ .
hadiths.en:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr al -Bazanti asked Imam al -Ridha {a.s} 
about a man who forgot to say Takbir al -Ihram (opening Takbir) until he had already 
said Takbir for bowing (ruku').  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It suffices him”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 343 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 144 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 353 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 915 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 16 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1000

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1002  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ذَّ إَّلأِسْي ِق ْي َّاحِ ن ِا ِجْذَّى  
وَّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ ي َّكْن ِي زَّة ً ي ُّم َّ ن َّسِي ث َّ إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زَّ كُّل َّهُّ إ َّوْ لَّمْ ي ُّكَّن ِّزْةُّ إ َّج ْز َّإ َّك َّ إَّلي  َّكْ ن ِي زُّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلُّ عَّن ْ ي َّكْن ِي زَّة ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ كُّلِّهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} who said:  
"I asked about a man who forgot the first Takbir of the prayer (Takbir al -Ihram).  
Imam {a.s} said: 'If he remembers it before the bowing (ruku'), he should say the Takbir, 
then recite, and then perform the ruku'. If he remembers it during the prayer, he should 
say the Takbir at the place of the Takbir, whether before or after the recitation.'  
I asked: 'What if he remembers it after the prayer?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'Then he should make it up, and there is nothing wrong with him.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 343 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 145 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 352 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 916 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 14 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1001
